Greek-Entry.English-Entry: νὴ τὸν Ἄρη, Ἀττικοὶ δὲ ποδοκάκη
anything flowing, running water, stream, spring
one whose limbs are too small for his body
in the form of a shrine
inmost part of a temple, shrine
preside at the festival of the ship
to be manager of a tenement-house
one who rented and sub-let tenement-houses
the chief official of a division
have the command of the sea
to be a shipbuilder, build ships,
famed for ships, famous by sea,
equipped as a ship, crossing the water,
one who keeps watch on board ship,
to be full of youthful spirit,
to be the younger of two,
act like a hot-headed youth, wilfully
the lowest of the three strings
wear a fawnskin at the feast of Dionysus,
to be changed into a fawn,
strife at law, dispute before a judge,
to be inundated by the Nile,
used in the graduation of the Nilometer,
festival on the inundation of the Nile,
one who breaks up a fallow,
vases dug out of the tombs of Corinth,
bear as a dead body to the burial
having to do with the dead,
rite by which ghosts were called up and questioned
feel just resentment, to be wroth at undeserved good
is indignant, ashamed at the thought of . . , rejects it as unseemly,
disposed to indignation at any one's undeserved good
have and hold as one's portion, possess,
lately made one of the people
a band of youths, the youth
one who ploughs up fallow land,
just come out of a vapour-bath,
new and sparkling, with all the gloss on,
newly drawn from a wound, bloody,
to be reared as in the nest,
having newly joined a sect, proselyte,
suffering from contraction of the tendons,
one who stitches with sinews, mender of shoes, cobbler,
one who makes strings for the lyre,
drawn and just ready to fly,
to be caught by drawing a string,
to be supplied with nerves, innervated,
sprung from Ixion and the cloud,
place where nets are set to catch birds,
sweep clean, clean out, plunder a temple,
haul a ship up on land,
one who hauls up a ship into dock,
official in charge of the temple-fabric,
to be overseer of a dockyard,
take the law into one's own hands,
attempt political changes, make revolutionary movements,
ἄλλως τε μέντοι ν. Δία πάντως καὶ
belonging to a ship, consisting of ships,
that which is spun, thread, yarn,
that will infallibly be put in force,
to be as a babe, childish,
to be childish, play like a child,
to be governor of an island
one who is fasting, on an empty stomach,
purify by a libation without wine,
to be sober, drink no wine,
tomb and temple of Seleucus I,
the prevailing opinion, vote of the majority,
it was the prevailing opinion that
likely to conquer, conducing to victory,
τὸ ὀρχούμενον τοῖς δακτύλοις ἐπιψοφεῖν: σεισμὸς Νιόβη,
that which is perceived, perception, thought,
ruler of the world of Intelligence,
by a marriage with an inferior,
child of a citizen father and an alien mother,
going in a row from pasture,
to be a shepherd, tend flocks,
one who explains laws, legal adviser,
to be furnished with laws, have a code of laws,
that which is in habitual practice, use
composition including both words and melody,
having the form of Intelligence, of intelligible character,
need medical attendance, to be sick,
skilled in judging of diseases by their symptoms
put aside for oneself, appropriate, purloin,
new moon : the first of the month,
one who celebrates the new moon,
she that rises and sets by night,
ubi homo neque matutino tempore videt neque vespertino,
incapacity to see except in bright light, night-blindness,
hunting by night, taking game asleep,
office of commander of the night-watch,
rising above the horizon after sunset and setting below it before sunrise
setting before sunset and rising after sunrise.
spell for making a woman talk in her sleep,
lead the bride to the bridegroom's house,
one who leads her from her home to the bridegroom's house,
one who negotiates a marriage for another,
she who escorts the bride, bridesmaid,
to be given in marriage, marry,
hollow between the under-lip and chin,
touch with a sharp point, prick, stab, pierce,
watch the night through, pass the night,
